title: Chemistry for Mortals
subtitle: An atomic look around you
author: Wordsus Team
description: A fascinating 52-week journey through the world of chemistry. From the structure of the atom to the most modern applications of nanotechnology, explained in a simple and entertaining way.

chapters.title: The Taste of Fire: The Maillard Reaction to the Rescue
chapters.description: Discover how the Maillard reaction transforms amino acids and sugars when heat is applied, creating that irresistible color and flavor in food.

chapters.title: Water and Oil: The Chemical Secret of Saponification
chapters.description: We explore the fascinating process of saponification, where soap molecules achieve the amazing feat of mixing water and oil together to clean.

chapters.title: The Smell of Rain: The Fascinating Chemistry of Petrichor
chapters.description: Immerse yourself in the scent of rain caused by geosmin and petrichor, chemical compounds detectable by humans in incredibly tiny amounts.

chapters.title: Fire on the Tongue: The Thermal Deception of Capsaicin
chapters.description: Meet capsaicin, the deceptive molecule in chili peppers that activates your tongue's pain receptors and makes you feel like you are burning.

chapters.title: Tears in the Kitchen: The Hidden Gas Inside Onions
chapters.description: We analyze syn-propanethial-S-oxide, the onion gas that reacts with the moisture in your eyes to form sulfuric acid and make you shed tears.

chapters.title: The Scent of the Past: Why We Love Old Book Smells
chapters.description: We reveal the secret of the old book smell: the slow chemical degradation of paper lignin that emits beautiful scents of vanilla and dried flowers.

chapters.title: Candy Geyser: The Hidden Physics Behind Nucleation
chapters.description: Understand the amazing physical-chemical nucleation that causes the famous geyser when you drop Mentos candies into diet cola soft drinks.

chapters.title: Magic in the Oven: Baking Soda vs. Baking Powder
chapters.description: Learn the chemistry behind baking soda and baking powder, acid-base reactions that release carbon dioxide to make your delicious cakes fluffy.

chapters.title: The Incredible Diaper: Superabsorbent Polymer Networks
chapters.description: Be amazed by superabsorbent polymers in diapers, sodium polyacrylate networks that trap massive amounts of water through hydrogen bonding.

chapters.title: Guilt-Free Coffee: The Supercritical Extraction Mystery
chapters.description: Discover how carbon dioxide is used in supercritical extraction to successfully remove caffeine from coffee without altering its great taste.

chapters.title: The Miracle Layer: Graphene and the Future of Materials
chapters.description: We explore the benefits of graphene, an incredibly thin layer of carbon that claims to be the strongest material and a fantastic conductor.

chapters.title: Frozen Smoke: The Incredible World of Magical Aerogels
chapters.description: Be fascinated by aerogels, where liquid is replaced by gas to create a silicon dioxide structure with incredible thermal insulating power.

chapters.title: Metal with Memory: The Thermal Journey of Nitinol
chapters.description: Meet nitinol, a revolutionary nickel and titanium alloy with shape memory that, when heat is applied, fully recovers its initial structure.

chapters.title: The Infinite Void: Vantablack and the Absence of Light
chapters.description: Marvel at Vantablack, a dense coating of aligned carbon nanotubes that absorbs visible light, making 3D objects look completely flat and 2D.

chapters.title: Stopping Bullets: The Amazing Bonds Seen in Kevlar
chapters.description: Discover the intricate network of hydrogen bonds between polymer chains that give Kevlar the incredible resistance needed to stop speeding bullets.

chapters.title: Liquid or Solid: Walking on Non-Newtonian Fluids
chapters.description: Experiment with non-Newtonian fluids like oobleck, where starch chains act as a solid under pressure and freely flow as a liquid when at rest.

chapters.title: The Non-Stick King: The Strong Bonds Inside Teflon
chapters.description: Learn why the carbon-fluorine bonds in Teflon make this molecule so incredibly inert that it refuses to react or stick to absolutely anything.

chapters.title: Extreme Glue: The Polymerization of Cyanoacrylates
chapters.description: We explore how cyanoacrylates act in superglue, instantly polymerizing with the moisture in the air instead of just drying out on their own.

chapters.title: Synthetic Ice: Ultra-High Molecular Weight Polyethylene
chapters.description: Be surprised by ultra-high molecular weight polyethylene, a plastic with huge chains that make it incredibly resistant to wear and friction.

chapters.title: The Melting Metal: Gallium Right in the Palm of Your Hand
chapters.description: Play mentally with gallium, a harmless metal whose uniquely low melting point causes it to turn into liquid just by holding it in your warm hands.

chapters.title: Alien Crystals: The Iridescent Beauty of Bismuth
chapters.description: Admire the fantastic, iridescent, and alien-looking crystals of bismuth, formed through a highly complex cooling and surface oxidation process.

chapters.title: The Liquid Metal: Mercury and the World of Amalgams
chapters.description: Dive into the properties of mercury, the only metal that remains a liquid at room temperature, and its fascinating ability to form amalgams.

chapters.title: Fleeting and Radioactive: The Hunt for Elusive Francium
chapters.description: Chase the rarity of francium, an alkali metal so unstable and highly radioactive that only a few grams exist in the entire Earth's crust.

chapters.title: Aquatic Explosion: The Dramatic Behavior of Sodium
chapters.description: Study the explosive nature of pure sodium, an alkali metal eager to give up its electron that reacts violently when coming into contact with water.

chapters.title: Diamond and Graphite: The Two Faces of Allotropic Carbon
chapters.description: Understand carbon allotropy and how the valuable diamond and soft graphite are chemically identical but have completely different atomic bonds.

chapters.title: Spontaneous Fire: The Hidden Danger in White Phosphorus
chapters.description: Travel back in time to the alchemist who discovered reactive white phosphorus by boiling urine, an element that naturally burns in the air.

chapters.title: Ephemeral Plastic: The Strange Case of Polymeric Sulfur
chapters.description: Discover the thermal transformation of polymeric sulfur, obtaining an elastic and plastic mass by rapidly cooling completely melted yellow sulfur.

chapters.title: Recyclable Pills: The Strange History of Antimony
chapters.description: Be amazed by the ancient perpetual pills of antimony, a toxic metalloid used as a medieval purge that was actually recycled for generations.

chapters.title: The Magic Clock: Time Delays with Chemical Iodine
chapters.description: Watch the magical iodine clock, an amazing clear solution that abruptly changes to a dark blue color after a fully predetermined amount of time.

chapters.title: Giant Foam: The Famous Elephant Toothpaste Experiment
chapters.description: Discover the catalytic decomposition of hydrogen peroxide that causes the explosive and intensely hot foam in the elephant toothpaste experiment.

chapters.title: Pulsating Colors: The Amazing Oscillating Chemical Clock
chapters.description: Be fascinated by the Belousov-Zhabotinsky chemical clock, one of the few oscillating reactions that rhythmically changes color without stopping.

chapters.title: Inextinguishable Fire: The Destructive Power of Thermite
chapters.description: Feel the red-hot heat of thermite, a highly dangerous mixture of aluminum powder and iron oxide that instantly burns at extreme temperatures.

chapters.title: Light without Heat: The Mystery of Chemiluminescence
chapters.description: Light up the dark by understanding chemiluminescence, where hydrogen peroxide and an ester release bright light without producing any heat.

chapters.title: Fake Ice: The Thermal Deception of Sodium Acetate
chapters.description: Trick your senses with the hot ice of sodium acetate, a supersaturated solution that crystallizes upon touch, releasing plenty of thermal energy.

chapters.title: Ghostly Footprints: The Revealing Glow of Luminol
chapters.description: Investigate how forensic experts act using luminol, a molecule that reacts to the iron inside blood hemoglobin to emit a ghostly blue glow.

chapters.title: Painted Sky: The Metals Behind Fireworks
chapters.description: Look up at the sky to understand emission spectra and how heating different metal salts generates those brilliant colors in amazing fireworks.

chapters.title: Mysterious Fog: The Cold Sublimation of Dry Ice
chapters.description: Walk through the dense fog caused by the sublimation of dry ice, a process where solid carbon dioxide completely bypasses the liquid state.

chapters.title: Silent Killer: The Lethal Embrace of Carbon Monoxide
chapters.description: Avoid the silent killer by understanding carbon monoxide and its potent, yet completely lethal affinity with hemoglobin that suffocates the body.

chapters.title: Cellular Arrest: How Cyanide Suffocates Our Body
chapters.description: Discover how the cyanide ion acts at a molecular level by fully blocking the cytochrome c oxidase enzyme and paralyzing energy in our cells.

chapters.title: Furious Nitrogen: The Destructive Chemistry behind TNT
chapters.description: Explore the terrifying chemistry behind TNT and nitroglycerin, where nitrogen atoms desperately seek to bond, releasing massive destructive energy.

chapters.title: Seed Poison: Ricin as a Molecular Saboteur
chapters.description: Understand the immense danger of ricin, a master plant toxin entirely capable of sabotaging and inactivating thousands of cellular ribosomes per minute.

chapters.title: Aesthetic Terror: The Lethality of Botulinum Toxin
chapters.description: Analyze the paralyzing power of the lethal botulinum toxin, a protein that fully blocks the nerve impulses that order your muscles to contract.

chapters.title: Absolute Corrosion: The Danger of Fluoroantimonic Acid
chapters.description: Discover the fearsome fluoroantimonic acid, a dangerous superacid capable of dissolving even glass that can uniquely be stored inside Teflon.

chapters.title: Fire in the Ashes: The Power of Chlorine Trifluoride
chapters.description: Be terrified by the highly oxidizing capacity of chlorine trifluoride, which can easily set fire to asbestos, glass, or even already burnt ashes.

chapters.title: Sweet and Poisonous: The Roman Fall by Lead Acetate
chapters.description: Review the tragic Roman fall linked to lead acetate, an artificial sweetener that silently intoxicated many unsuspecting people with heavy metals.

chapters.title: The World's Bread: Salvation by the Haber-Bosch Process
chapters.description: Appreciate the Haber-Bosch process, which converts inert nitrogen into ammonia for fertilizers, being the key chemical reaction sustaining humanity.

chapters.title: Purple for Everyone: The Casual Synthesis of Mauveine
chapters.description: Relive the fortuitous discovery of mauveine, where a young chemist's attempt to cure malaria resulted in the world's first synthetic purple dye.

chapters.title: Indestructible Rubber: The Vulcanization Accident
chapters.description: Be thankful for the chance of vulcanization, where adding sulfur and heat to natural rubber produced cross-links that revolutionized all tires.

chapters.title: The King Analgesic: The Hidden History of Aspirin
chapters.description: Discover the origin of the most popular drug after the chemical modification of willow extract into acetylsalicylic acid, also known as aspirin.

chapters.title: Capturing Light: The Ancient Halide Photography
chapters.description: Focus your senses on ancient silver halide photography, where light photons hit a film, reducing ions into solid metal to capture classic photos.

chapters.title: Dividing the Crude: Magic in Fractional Distillation
chapters.description: Learn the usefulness of fractional distillation to separate hydrocarbons from petroleum, obtaining derivatives from sticky asphalt to gasoline.

chapters.title: Infinite Batteries: The Impact of Lithium-Ion
chapters.description: Celebrate the energy revolution of lithium-ion batteries and their reversible reaction that allows you to charge and use your mobile devices.
